violations.text: Rice cooker lid was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
Food was observed stored in non-food grade containers in the walk-in. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025
A single-use bottle was observed being used as a scoop in a container of rice. Staff disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
Lids to containers in walk-in were observed cracked, with pieces missing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -bottom shelf of reach-in freezers -doors to prep unit and refrigerators. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025

The ventilation hood in the kitchen area has an accumulation of grease residue and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Must reinstate. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2024

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/22/2024
Food observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Must reinstate. New Violation. Correct By: 07/20/2024
The hand-washing sink in the toilet room was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

Tofu, garlic in oil, and eggs observed held at 59-90 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Tofu had just been delivered within 30 minutes and was placed in walk in cooler. Eggs and garlic were disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

Chicken was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed hot in the prep unit in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 06/22/2024

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler and kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Scoops observed in some containers of dry ingredients with the handles in the food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
Single-use items observed being used for food preparation and storage. Single-use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
Several plastic containers and lids used for food storage observed not in good repair, cracked with pieces missing. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:refrigerators prep unitsprep tables including legswalk in shelvesrice cookersThe freezer and chest freezer have an accumulation of ice, frost, and food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in areas of the kitchen. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
